steps.title: Set equations equal and solve for x
steps.content: At the intersection point, both equations yield the same $y$-value. Set them equal: $$(x-1)^2 = 2x - 3$$ Expand the left side: $$x^2 - 2x + 1 = 2x - 3$$ Rearrange: $$x^2 - 4x + 4 = 0$$ This is a perfect square trinomial: $$(x-2)^2 = 0$$ Therefore, $x = 2$.

steps.title: Substitute to find the y-coordinate
steps.content: Using $y = 2x - 3$: $$y = 2(2) - 3 = 4 - 3 = 1$$ The $y$-coordinate of the intersection point is 1.
